_note: Manual in-game crafting difficulty-color observations, used to reverse-engineer recipe trivials the wiki is missing (it shows '?'). Difficulty colors easiest->hardest: green (= trivial, no more skill-ups), lightblue, darkblue, white (~50% success), yellow, orange, red. A recipe seen GREEN at skill S has trivial <= S; any other color means trivial > S. Recording the same recipe at several skill levels pins the exact trivial (it turns green at its trivial) and calibrates each colour's skill-gap. Edit this file as you hit new skill milestones; the site merges it over the wiki recipe data.

_model.note: Trivials land on MULTIPLES OF 5 (in-game breakpoints are every 5 levels), and each colour band is 5 skill points wide. This single model reproduces BOTH the skill-48 and skill-50 readings exactly for every recipe, so trivial estimates below are now confident to the nearest 5 (red items are a lower bound until they shift colour).

Blacksmithing.note: All copper recipes not listed here were green/trivial at skill 48. Enchanted variants matched their base item's colour.
greenTrivialAtOrBelow: (and every other copper recipe not listed above)

Blacksmithing.note: +2 skill from the level-48 reading. Confirms the multiples-of-5 / 5-wide-band model: the level-48 lightblue recipes are now exactly green (trivial 50). Enchanted Copper Plate Helm reads green here too (matches its base; the earlier 'lightblue' look was a misread). Enchanted variants match their base item's colour.
greenTrivialAtOrBelow: (and every other copper recipe not listed above)

trivialEstimates._method: Derived from the colour at skill 48 AND skill 50 via the multiples-of-5 / 5-wide-band model in _model, which reproduces both readings exactly. Values are the nearest multiple of 5; red-at-50 items are a lower bound (>= 80) until a higher-skill reading shifts their colour. Enchanted variants share their base item's trivial.
